observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant exert all reasonable efforts to reconstruct the
SLs pertaining to the cash accountability of the former Municipal Treasurer,
based on the availability of reliable records, in compliance with Sections
111(1) and 114(2) of PD No. 1445. The reconstructed SLs should be
submitted to the Audit Team for verification and audit; and
b. Office of the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the OIC-Municipal
Accountant, review and reconcile the available cashbooks with the
accounting records. A reconciliation report, along with complete and
updated copies of the cashbooks, should be submitted to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the:
a. Management (i) direct the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and other AOs
concerned to exert all reasonable efforts to retrieve the source documents
supporting various book reconciling items and submit the same to the Office
of the Municipal Accountant; and (ii) pursue previous requests with the
depository bank to obtain copies of debit and credit memoranda related to
the said reconciling items for proper recording in the books of accounts; and
b. Office of the Municipal Treasurer (i) perform reconciliation of all Cash in
Bank cashbooks with the corresponding accounting records; (ii) submit a
detailed reconciliation report to the Audit Team; and (iii) ensure strict
compliance with the monthly reconciliation of cashbook balances with
subsidiary accounting records, in accordance with Section 63(F) of the
LTOM.

We recommended that the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant (i) continue sending regular written notices to all
AOs concerned at least quarterly to demand the settlement of their
outstanding cash advances; and (ii) ensure that no new cash advance is
granted unless previous ones have been fully liquidated; and
b. Management (i) initiate the preparation and consolidation of necessary
documents in support of the request for write-off of deceased employees and
those dormant unliquidated cash advances; and (ii) consider the filing of
appropriate legal or administrative action against AOs who have deliberately
failed to liquidate their advances after proper demand, if warranted.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the members of the Inventory Committee to promptly conduct
physical count of inventories and prepare the corresponding RPCI in
compliance with existing rules;
b. Facilitate the reconciliation of the results of the physical count with the
accounting records to enable the recording of necessary adjustments and
ensure the accurate valuation of inventories in the books of accounts; and
c. Institute measures to ensure the timely conduct of annual physical inventory
and submission of the corresponding report.

We recommended that the relevant offices (i) revisit the provisions of the
aforementioned Circulars; and (ii) ensure compliance with preparation and
maintenance of the prescribed forms, registry, and reports related to Semi-
Expendable Property.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Property and Accounting Units to immediately reconcile the
RPCPPE with their records in compliance with Section 6.3 of COA Circular
No. 2020-006;
b. Facilitate the disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPE items
pursuant to Section 7.0 of the same circular;
c. Thereafter, undertake the derecognition procedures for the one-time
cleansing of non-existing/missing PPE items without available record of
accountability, following the provisions of Section 8.0 of the circular; and
d. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-GSO to review and
reinforce the observance of proper accounting and reporting procedures for all
PPE-related transactions (e.g., acquisition, receipt, issuance, transfer, and
disposal), in line with applicable laws and regulations. The Municipal Mayor
is likewise encouraged to regularly monitor and ensure that annual physical
inventory is conducted and the corresponding reports are properly submitted
on time.

We reiterated that Management:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of local
road networks and ensure that the report is provided with detailed
breakdown and cost segregation of each road component;
b. Thereafter, instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the
OIC-Municipal Engineer and the OIC-GSO, to reconcile the RPCLRN with
the appropriate records and prepare the journal voucher to update the Road
Networks account in the books of accounts;
c. Enjoin the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Acting GSO to maintain
complete and updated LRNLC and LRNPC, respectively, in accordance with
53
the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008; and
d. Ensure that a complete physical count of local road networks is undertaken
annually, and the reports thereon are submitted to the Audit Team and the
OIC-Municipal Accountant not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to provide the OIC-
Municipal Accountant with a list of completed projects, duly supported by
Certificates of Completion and Final Acceptance, as basis for adjusting the
balances of CIP and related asset accounts; and
b. Direct the Municipal Engineer to consistently monitor ongoing projects and
promptly submit the required reports and documentation to the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to facilitate the timely transfer of completed projects
to the appropriate asset accounts.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant (i) conduct a
thorough review and reconciliation of the Due to NGAS account, ensuring that all
SLs are complete, updated, and contain sufficient details to support the recorded
balances; (ii) investigate and resolve the negative and unreconciled balances and
make the necessary adjustments, if warranted, to correct the discrepancies; (iii)
strictly observe the provisions of Section 111(1) of PD No. 1445 and the applicable
standards under IPSAS 1 in the maintenance of accounting records and
preparation of financial statements to promote fair presentation and
accountability.

We further recommended that Management (i) facilitate the preparation of
liquidation reports and ensure the return of unutilized balances from completed
PPAs to their respective source agencies; (ii) give priority to the completion of PPAs
experiencing delays or deferred implementation; and (iii) initiate the preparation
and consolidation of necessary documents in support of the request for write-off of
dormant fund transfers.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC Secretariat immediately submit the lacking contract documents to the
Audit Team to facilitate the necessary auditorial, legal, and technical review.
Otherwise, continued non-compliance will warrant the recommendation for
the filing of appropriate criminal and administrative charges against the
responsible officers; and
b. Management enjoin the BAC Secretariat to strictly adhere with COA
Circular No. 2009-001 on the timely and complete submission of copies of
government contracts to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management consider the potential benefits of COA
Circular No. 2023-006 by transitioning to the digital submission of ERDs to
enhance document security and accessibility.

We recommended that the LSB enhance its efforts to ensure strict and
diligent compliance with the timely preparation, submission, and posting of
utilization reports, in accordance with Item 6.1 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1,
series of 2017.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Mun. Accountant to continue in reconstructing the SLs of the Cash - Local Treasury account for each AO depending on the availability of reliable records and reconcile the same with the General Ledger (GL) to effect adjustments in the books and accurately support the balance of the said account in the financial statements in accordance with Sections 111(1) and 114(2) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445; and
b. Require the Mun. Treasurer to regularly reconcile his cashbooks with the accounting records so that discrepancies or errors could be immediately identified and corrected, in compliance with Section 63(F) of the LTOM.
observations.managementAction: The Mun. Accountant has substantially reconstructed the SLs.
observations.reason: Reconciliation with the accounting records is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
(a) Mun. Mayor (i) require the Mun. Treasurer and the Mun. Accountant to immediately reconcile their records and submit a reconciliation statement thereon and take-up the necessary adjusting entries in their respective records; (ii) demand from/require the former and incumbent Mun. Treasurers to immediately submit to the Mun. Accountant the source documents of the unrecorded disbursements and deposits, and other book reconciling items, after which, the same shall be submitted to the Audit Team for audit purposes; and (iii) make representations with the bank to furnish the Municipality with copies of debit and credit memos of said reconciling items for recording in the books of accounts. 
(b) Mun. Treasurer (i) strictly observe the provisions of Sections 63(D) and 63(F) of the LTOM on the maintenance of complete cashbooks and the reconciliation of cashbook balances with subsidiary accounting records as this is an important control tool for cash; and (ii) monitor long outstanding checks on a regular basis and notify the payees concerned to return the stale checks for the issuance of replacement checks in accordance with Section 83(b)(4) of the LTOM; and Mun. Accountant further investigate and provide details for all reconciling items in the BRS and thereafter make the necessary adjustments in the books.
observations.managementAction: Require reconciliation and adjusting entries by the Mun. Treasurer and OIC-Mun. Accountant; sent request letter to the Acting Mun. Treasurer for the submission of all supporting documents. The Acting Mun. Treasurer and OIC-Mun. Accountant are coordinating and conducting reconciliation of cashbooks with accounting records. The Acting Mun. Treasurer requested the bank for debit and credit memoranda.
observations.reason: Unavailability of supporting documents and ongoing reconciliation; some reconciling items still awaiting supporting documents and verification.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the:
a. Mun. Accountant (i) regularly serve written notices to all concerned accountable officers (AOs) to demand settlement of their outstanding cash advances; (ii) consider withholding the salaries of AOs still connected with the Municipality who refuse to settle their accounts without justifiable reason; (iii) continuously impose strict internal control over granting of cash advances to ensure proper settlement of previous ones before granting any additional cash advances; and (iv) exert efforts in determining the causes of negative cash advance balances and thereafter effect the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts, as appropriate; and
b. Mun. Mayor (i) request for write-off of the accounts of deceased employees and those dormant unliquidated cash advances pursuant to the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023; and (ii) initiate the filing of appropriate action against the erring AOs who still failed to settle their accounts after demand, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Mun. Accountant has issued demand letters to AOs concerned and reminded those still in service to settle their outstanding balances, warning that salaries may be withheld for non-compliance. Reimbursement of travel expenses and release of bonuses are being withheld until existing cash advances are settled. The Mun. Accountant has refunded several advances and is reviewing related records. Some AOs responded to the demand letters.
observations.reason: Legal action was not pursued, as the AOs have responded to the demand letters.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the members of the Inventory Committee to promptly conduct physical count of inventories and prepare the corresponding RPCI in compliance with existing rules; 
b. Facilitate the reconciliation of the results of the physical count with the accounting records to enable the recording of necessary adjustments and ensure the accurate valuation of inventories in the books of accounts; and

observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee will conduct a physical count of inventories, reconcile the results with accounting records, and implement measures including preparing an inventory plan, relieving committee members of other duties, and ensuring timely submission of required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the relevant offices (i) revisit the provisions of the aforementioned Circulars; and (ii) ensure compliance with preparation and maintenance of the prescribed forms, registry, and reports related to Semi-Expendable Property.
observations.managementAction: The GSO, in coordination with the Municipal Accounting Office, will ensure compliance with the preparation and maintenance of the required forms, registries, and reports related to semi-expendable property.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Mun. Mayor:
a. Require the Property and Accounting Units to undertake collaborative procedures for the reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with the records of the offices concerned pursuant to Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; 
b. Cause the conduct of disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPE items in accordance with Section 7.0 of the said circular; and

We also recommended that the Mun. Accountant and the OIC-GSO ensure that proper accounting and reporting procedures for all acquisition/receipt/issue/transfer/disposal of PPE pursuant to the relevant laws, rules and regulations on property and supply management are strictly followed. observations.managementAction: Reconciliation efforts for PPE records are ongoing. Procedures to dispose of non-existent or missing items are being followed and derecognition steps will be undertaken in accordance with Section 7.0 of COA Circular 2020-006. Management commits to accurate recording of PPE-related transactions and is seeking funding to acquire a Property Management and Supply System for improved tracking. Annual physical inventories will be conducted and reported as required.
observations.reason: Enormous paperwork; measures such as presence of inventory plan, relief of members of Inventory Committee from other tasks to dedicate their time to conduct inventory and submission of the corresponding reports are in effect; reconciliation with accounting records is still in progress.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Mun. Accountant:
a. Verify the validity of dormant and unsubstantiated payables and confirm the existence of actual claimants. Legitimate claims should be settled promptly, while unresolved payables may be subject to reversal if found to lack substantiation; and
b. Investigate the reasons for negative balances and make appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts to rectify discrepancies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality verified and resolved outstanding payables; certification from concerned offices with outstanding payable were issued and resolved. Adjustments were already made in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Mun. Accountant (i) conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of all dormant fund transfers; (ii) effect adjustment in the books for the correction of identified errors/misstatements in the said account; and (iii) cause the submission of liquidation reports to the source agency for all fully implemented projects, programs and activities (PPAs).
We also recommended that the Mun. Mayor (i) cause the return of unexpended balances of completed PPAs to its source agency; (ii) prioritize the completion of PPAs with delayed or postponed implementation; and (iii) consider filing a request for authority to write-off dormant fund transfers to this office following the guidelines and procedures under COA Circular No. 2023-008.
observations.managementAction: The Mun. Accountant conducted regular and periodic verification of dormant fund transfers and effected adjustments in the books; some reconciliations are still ongoing. The Municipality facilitated the return of unexpended balances of completed PPAs to source agencies and prioritized completion of delayed PPAs. Request for write-off documents are not yet available.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still ongoing; some documents needed for request for write-off are not yet available and some balances are subject to reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Requisitioning Units of drugs and medicines ensure that their PRs are provided with complete details and specifications of the items requested and accompanied by a certification that the items being requisitioned conform with the PNF; 
b. Requisitioning Units and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) (i) provide justification for their non-reference in the DPRI as the ceiling price in the PRs and POs, and (ii) ensure compliance with RA No. 9502 and DOH AO No. 2019-0040; and
c. Mun. Accountant (i) coordinate with the offices concerned relative to the submission of the lacking documentary requirements, (ii) ensure that disbursements are supported with complete documentations, and (iii) strictly observe the perpetual inventory method of accounting for inventories.
observations.managementAction: PRs were provided with complete details and specifications; BAC always checks the price of medicines in the PRs with reference to the DPRI as the ceiling price. The OIC-Mun. Accountant coordinated with concerned offices for the submission of lacking documentary requirements and ensured disbursements are supported with complete documentations. Perpetual inventory method was observed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Cause the submission to the Audit Team of Monthly Report of Official Travels of each government vehicle and List of all serviceable Mun. vehicles with information on its plate/chassis/engine numbers and odometer status, certified by the OIC-GSO; 
b. Ensure the issuance of completely filled out Fuel/Oil Withdrawal Slips and Driver’s Trip Tickets; and 
c. Impose additional controls to ensure that FOL is used solely for public purpose and that the same is not diverted to any public use other than that provided by law or ordinance.
observations.managementAction: The Acting GSO submits monthly report of official travels of each government vehicle and list of all serviceable Mun. vehicles with information on its plate/chassis/engine numbers and odometer status, certified by the OIC-GSO. The issuance of completely filled out Fuel/Oil Withdrawal Slips and Driver’s Trip Tickets is ensured. Additional controls to ensure FOL is used solely for public purpose are implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consider the development of comprehensive guidelines that clearly define the criteria, eligibility, application process, approval procedures, and documentary requirements for the grant of financial assistance and in-kind donations under the Community Development Services Program.
observations.managementAction: Consideration for the development of specific guidelines for CDSP was undertaken; the Management included specific guidelines for CDSP in the Local Expenditure recommendation for CY 2025. Management practices fiscal discipline and austerity measures to prioritize government transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Cause the immediate formulation and execution of a comprehensive and detailed catch-up plan for the full implementation of all priority development projects at the soonest possible time and consider realignment or reprogramming of the unimplemented PPAs to other feasible priority development projects; 
b. Require the MPDC, in coordination with the Mun. Engineer, to re-assess the selected mode of implementation of projects in the AIP to identify the most efficient and appropriate mode for the Municipality; and 
c. Ensure that the status report of government PPAs is completely provided with the required information (e.g. particulars, date started, no. of extensions, target completion date, costs, etc.).
observations.managementAction: Management reviewed and reprogrammed savings from completed projects and monitored and assessed PPAs; conducted meetings to regularly assess the PPAs funded and implemented. The Municipality is prioritizing completion of delayed PPAs.
observations.reason: Target completion dates for some PPAs were still not indicated in the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Mun. Mayor:
(i) ensure the balanced implementation of activities addressing the four thematic areas of DRRM by making sure that implementation of prevention and mitigation projects and activities are also given utmost priority; (ii) expedite the prompt implementation and completion of DRRM projects, in actively coordination with the Mun. DRRM Council and other offices concerned; and (iii) cause the review of continuing projects under the Local DRRM Fund to determine whether they are still part of the vital needs of the Municipality otherwise reprogram them to other priority PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Reprogramming of the remaining balances to priority projects was implemented. Management is actively pursuing land donation finalization with the Department of Agrarian Reform for the construction/establishment of Narra Disaster Resiliency Complex. Appropriation of TF and Continuing Appropriations were reprogrammed to various disaster preparedness activities through Ordinance No. 1474 and Resolution No. 5360.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, in coordination with the members of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) and other stakeholders, develop robust monitoring and reporting mechanisms to track the progress of GAD PPAs. Implementing regular reviews and evaluations will allow for ongoing assessment of performance against objectives and identification of areas for improvement, ultimately facilitating the successful completion of GAD PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Management, in coordination with the members of the GFPS, intensified monitoring and reporting mechanisms to track the progress of GAD PPAs. Measures were undertaken to fast-track project implementation and ensure effective achievement of GAD program objectives.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant support the general ledger controlling account with complete and updated subsidiary records to effectively control and monitor any movement in the Land account. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, review the discrepancies in their records for its reconciliation and the subsequent adjustment of the Land account balance. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor facilitate the immediate titling of real properties owned by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconstruct the SLs of
the Cash Local Treasury account for each AO and reconcile the same with the GL to
accurately support the balance of the said account in the financial statements in
accordance with Sections 111(1) and 114(2) of PD No. 1445.

Thereafter, the Municipal Treasurer should regularly reconcile his
cashbooks with the accounting records so that discrepancies or errors could be
immediately identified and corrected, in compliance with Section 63(F) of the
LTOM.

We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Mayor (i) require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant to immediately reconcile their records as at December 31, 2021
and submit a reconciliation statement thereon and draw the necessary
adjusting entries in their respective records; (ii) demand from/require the
Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to the Municipal Accountant the
source documents of the unrecorded disbursements and deposits, and other
book reconciling items, after which, the same shall be submitted to the Audit
Team for audit purposes; and (iii) make representations with the bank to
furnish the Municipality with copies of debit and credit memoranda of said
reconciling items for recording in the books of accounts;

b. Municipal Treasurer (i) strictly observe the provisions of Sections 63(D) and
63(F) of the LTOM on the required maintenance of complete and updated
cashbooks in all accounts of all funds and the reconciliation of cashbook
balances with subsidiary accounting records as this is an important control
tool for cash; and (ii) monitor long outstanding checks on a regular basis and
notify the payees concerned to return the stale checks for the issuance of
replacement checks in accordance with Section 83(b)(4) of the LTOM; and

c. Municipal Accountant (i) immediately prepare and submit to the Audit
Team the lacking BRS for the CIB-LCSA; and (ii) further investigate and
provide details for all reconciling items in the BRS and thereafter make the
necessary adjustments in the books.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor (a) thru, the
Municipal Accountant, regularly serve written notice to AOs to demand
settlement of their outstanding cash advances; (b) consider withholding the
salaries of AOs still connected with the Municipality who refuse to settle their
accounts without justifiable reason; and (c) institute the necessary legal action
against those who failed to settle their accountabilities despite demand, if
warranted.

The Municipal Accountant exert efforts to locate documents and previous
records to facilitate reconciliation and/or determination of the causes of the
unreconciled and negative cash advance balances and thereafter effect the
necessary adjustment in the books of accounts, as appropriate.

In all instances, the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant must
ensure that the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 are strictly observed in
the granting and liquidation of cash advances and see that all cash advances are
fully liquidated at the end of the year.

We also advised the Municipal Mayor to file a request for authority to write-
off dormant unliquidated cash advances to this office pursuant to the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make written representations
with the management of the IAs and NGOs/POs to inform them of the unliquidated
fund transfers for the reconciliation of their records by requesting them to submit
reports and other necessary documents supporting the fund utilization and return
the unexpended balances, if any, and consider amending the MOA with the
barangays to expressly state the prescribed period on the submission of
liquidation reports for barangays who still have unliquidated fund transfers to
date. Otherwise, the Municipal Mayor should demand the immediate settlement
of all outstanding balances of barangays. Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant
should record the liquidation in the books of accounts and ensure that the
barangays have no existing unliquidated balances in the books of accounts
before certifying the DVs for subsequent fund transfers.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in pursuance of the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005, (i) conduct regular and periodic
verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of unliquidated fund
transfers to IAs and NGOs/POs; (ii) effect adjustment in the books by providing
separate SL per agency and purpose of the fund transfer in compliance with
Section 111(1) of PD No. 1445, and segregating the unsupported,
documented and unidentified balances; and (iii) ensure that the balances
reported in the SLs are consistent with the details in the Status Reports
submitted to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant should also prepare
aging of dormant inter-agency fund transfers on a quarterly basis to support the
request for write-off.

The Municipal Mayor is further advised to file a request for authority from
the COA to write-off dormant fund transfers, supported with the required
documents, to facilitate the cleansing of the books for the fair presentation of
receivable accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee expedite the reconciliation
of the RPCPPE with pertinent records, and preparation and submission of reports
for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in accordance with the provisions of
COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-GSO
ensure that proper accounting and reporting procedures for all
acquisition/receipt/issue/transfer/disposal of PPE pursuant to the relevant laws,
rules and regulations on property and supply management are strictly followed. The
Municipal Mayor should likewise ensure that physical inventory is undertaken
annually and reports thereon are submitted accordingly.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee provide the detailed
breakdown and cost segregation of each road component in the RPCLRN and
submit the same to the Municipal Accountant. Thereafter, the Municipal
Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and the OIC-GSO, should
reconcile the local road inventory report and road map with those recorded in the
RPI and prepare the journal voucher to update the Road Networks account in the
books of accounts. The Municipal Accountant and the OIC-GSO should likewise
maintain the relevant records required by COA Circular No. 2015-008.

The Municipal Mayor should ensure that the physical count of local road
networks is undertaken annually, and the reports thereon are submitted to the
Audit Team and the Municipal Accountant not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct periodic
verification of inter-agency fund transfers and reconciliation of the balances
between the source and implementing government entities concerned pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2016-005. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should provide
separate SL per agency and purpose of the fund transfer in compliance with Section
111(1) of PD No. 1445. The remaining account balance with no available records to
support the same should be clearly identified in the SL and an aging of dormant
inter-agency fund transfers should be prepared on a quarterly basis to support the
request for write-off.

Thereafter, the Municipal Mayor is advised to file a request for authority to
write-off dormant fund transfers to this office, supported with the required
documents, to facilitate the cleansing of the books for the fair presentation of
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipality revisit the existing policy on the
granting of telephone/communication expenses to its officials and employees and
take into consideration the most economical means currently available in the
market. We also advised the Municipality to consider Appendix 21 of Republic Act
(RA) No. 9184 on the appropriate method for the procurement of
telecommunications and internet service providers.

The Municipal Accountant, as the internal control unit, is also reminded to
ensure that no expenditure that falls under irregular, unnecessary, excessive,
extravagant and unconscionable as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-003 shall be
processed and paid to ensure that prudent use of government resources is strictly
observed.

We recommended that the BAC ensure that the issuance of resolution
recommending award of contract for procurements done through any of the
alternative methods of procurement is strictly followed, in compliance with the
provisions of Annex H on the Consolidated Guidelines for the Alternative
Methods of Procurement. The Municipal Mayor as the Head of Procuring Entity
(HoPE) should only award the contract to the supplier upon written
recommendation of the BAC.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the
completeness of supporting documents before approving payments thereof.

We recommended that a) the OIC-GSO and the BAC Secretariat
immediately submit the contract documents and POs for CYs 2020 to 2021 to the
Audit Team for the conduct of auditorial, legal and technical review; and b) the
Municipal Mayor enjoin the personnel concerned to strictly comply with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the timely submission of copies of
government contracts and POs to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to exhaust all possible means to obtain the supporting documents of the unrecorded disbursements and bank credit memoranda, and immediately transmit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording thereof in the books of accounts. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to make the necessary adjustments in the books for the long outstanding/stale checks in accordance with Section 189 of GAAM, Volume I, and Section 83(b)(4) of the LTOM.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate a regular employee who will monitor compliance by the Inventory Committee, the OIC-GSO, and the Municipal Accountant in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 on the procedural guidelines for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances. After the cut-off date set by the Inventory Committee in the PIP, the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-GSO should ensure that the proper accounting and reporting procedures for all acquisition/receipt/issue/transfer/disposal of PPE pursuant to the relevant laws, rules and regulations on property and supply management are strictly followed. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Engineer (i) require project engineers and personnel to be prudent in overseeing timely implementation; (ii) assist MPDC in identifying projects not yet completed or started for possible realignment or reprogramming; (iii) provide complete information on the status of projects; and (iv) provide satisfactory explanation for delays; and c. MPDC (i) ensure close coordination with Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant for proper monitoring and provision of necessary information in the Status Report of Government PPAs before submission to the Audit Team; (ii) conduct intensive review of continuing projects funded under the 20% DF to determine whether PPAs are still part of the vital needs of the Municipality otherwise reprogram them to other priority PPAs; and (iii) continue to identify development projects that are doable and more beneficial to the constituents.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant (i) submit the complete required documents to support the payments for consultancy services pursuant to CSC-COA-DBM JMC No. 2, series of 1979 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 and henceforth, see that payments for consultancy services are supported with adequate documentation; and (ii) ensure the proper withholding of taxes from the payment of consultancy services in accordance with Section 2.57.2(a)(6) of Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Municipal Human Resources Office, prioritize and consider filling–up the vacant positions by hiring new employees or expedite promotion of deserving employees to enhance its services to attain effective, and efficient full implementation of programs, projects and activities of the Municipality for the optimal benefit of its constituents as well as to provide equal job opportunities to the community.
Further, personnel movement has to be effected in order to acknowledge the exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby allowing the entry of new applicants for lower level positions.

We recommended that the HRMO (a) re-evaluate the organizational structure and staffing pattern of the Municipality taking into consideration its service requirements and financial capability, subject to the minimum standards and guidelines prescribed by the Civil Service Commission; (b) if warranted, extend Management efforts in filling-up the vacant plantilla positions; and (c) ensure that Job Orders and Contracts of Service cover only lump sum work or services such as janitorial, security, or consultancy where no employer-employee relationship exists between the individual and the government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002. We recommended the Municipal Accountant to: a. Revise the Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances taking into consideration the needed information presented above and submit to the Audit Team for evaluation; b. Reclassify to the Due to Officers and Employees those negative cash advances; c. Include in the checklist of documents the noted deficiencies and implement the same in the processing of cash advances and liquidations; d. Process only the travels of JO and COS with justifiable reasons and indorsement from their Department Head in compliance with DILG MC No. 2004-130, otherwise the same will be disallowed in audit; e. Discontinue the practice of partial liquidation of cash advances; and f. Consider withholding of salaries in case of unjustified failure of accountable officers to liquidate their cash advance within the prescribed period, without prejudice to the filing of appropriate charges, if warranted. We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to: a. Categorize the cash advance of the DO as to the type of expenditure e.g. payroll, financial assistance, other personnel benefits; per fund; and per special activity of the Municipality; with detailed estimates of the expenditures; b. Discontinue the practice of transferring the cash advance of the DO to another employee for the latter’s travelling expenses; and c. Discontinue the payment for the following expenses out of the cash advance of the DO, these should have been directly paid through check to the payee: • Withdrawal of the supplier’s 10% warranty • Fund Transfer • Monetization of Leave Credits • Barangay’s share in Real Property Taxes • Utility Expenses • Procurement of miscellaneous expenses done through the alternative method of procurement. We also recommended the Management to strictly adopt the “no liquidation, no cash advance” policy.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reviewed the Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances and withheld the salaries of those employees with outstanding cash advances. “No liquidation, no cash advance” policy is now being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to exert efforts for the immediate liquidation of the mentioned cash advances.
